prompt: I sell Honeycrisp apples at my farm stand. Create a simple item listing that shows the price, weight, and where they were grown.
Now turn my stand into a produce list with apples, tomatoes, and herbs, each showing price and available quantity.
Build a produce collection view that groups my items into browsable categories like fruits, vegetables, and herbs.
Build my complete farm stand module with produce collections, my farm name and logo, and a simple contact method for customers.
Build a live local market view that connects my stand to the other nearby vendors so shoppers can browse the market around me.
New vendors just arrived at the market. Update the shared market view so shoppers can see the added stands around mine.
I'm packing up for the day. Remove my stand cleanly from the live market view.
Show me the private inventory view for my stand, including unit cost, supplier details, stock counts, and profit margin.
Create a supplier-facing view that shows what I currently have available for wholesale without exposing my private internal data.
Create the public customer view of my stand for someone browsing from their phone at the market.
I'm at the park and want to see what people nearby are reading right now in a temporary location-based view.
When I walk into the library, automatically connect my reading list to the nearby shared reading space.
Build a module that watches nearby public nodes, shows what services they expose, and lets me browse them by type.
When I arrive at a pop-up market, assemble a live view of the stands around me and remove them as they leave.
Build a location-based public art exhibition feed where artists connect their portfolios when they are near the park screens.
Create a dietary overlay view for a live farmers market browser so I can see what fits my restrictions while I shop.
Build a city-planning heatmap dashboard that shows fresh food availability across active farmers markets in the metro area.
Build a produce item card for a market module with name, price, quantity, growing notes, and availability status.
Create a compact vendor summary strip that shows stand name, distance, open status, and the top items available right now.
Build a category switcher for a market browser so I can jump between fruit, vegetables, herbs, baked goods, and prepared foods.
Create a local presence toggle that lets a stand join or leave an emergent market network and shows current connection status.
Build a boundary panel for a market node where I decide what stays private, what suppliers can see, and what shoppers can browse publicly.
